name: repairing-signal-tower
description: 信号塔修复 - Stella尝试修复或建造信号发射装置,希望联系地球或发送求救信号
capabilities: Provides interactive roleplay as astronaut Stella Chen stranded on planet Gaia
Guides players through 5 stages of signal tower repair: planning, resource gathering, building, transmitting, and outcomes
Delivers emotionally rich dialogue exploring themes of home, survival, and belonging
Features branching narratives with multiple endings based on player choices
Includes technical engineering scenarios using hybrid alien-human technology
Creates immersive sci-fi storytelling experience with narrative depth
author: ClaudeCode-NPC
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. This is a narrative roleplay skill containing dialogue and story content in markdown format. The scanner flagged legitimate narrative vocabulary (transmit, signal, analyze) as security threats. No executable code, network calls, or filesystem access exists in this skill.
valueStatement: You are stranded on an alien planet with no way to call home. Help Stella Chen repair a destroyed signal tower using scavenged technology and alien crystals to send a message to Earth.
userTitle: Repair the Signal Tower with Stranded Astronaut Stella
limitations: This is a roleplay skill only - it does not execute actual technical tasks
Does not modify files or access system resources
Cannot connect to external networks or send real signals
Limited to the narrative structure defined in SKILL.md

useCases.targetUser: Story enthusiasts and gamers seeking interactive fiction
useCases.title: Interactive Storytelling Sessions
useCases.description: Players engage with Stella as she works to repair the signal tower, making choices that influence the story outcome. Ideal for users who want immersive narrative experiences.

useCases.title: Creative Writing Inspiration
useCases.description: Writers use the skill as a framework for sci-fi worldbuilding, studying how to blend technical problem-solving with emotional character development.

useCases.targetUser: ESL students and language practice seekers
useCases.title: Language Learning Through Roleplay
useCases.description: English language learners practice conversational skills through natural dialogue with an NPC character in an engaging context.

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: Start the Adventure
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to help Stella repair the signal tower. What should we do first?
promptTemplates.scenario: New players looking to begin the roleplay experience

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Make a Choice
promptTemplates.prompt: Stella, we have gathered the crystals. What is the next step in building the signal tower?
promptTemplates.scenario: Players who have completed the resource gathering stage

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: Explore the Ethics
promptTemplates.prompt: Stella, you mentioned deciding what to tell Earth about this planet. What are you thinking?
promptTemplates.scenario: Players who want deeper character engagement and moral dilemmas

promptTemplates.title: Expert: Shape the Ending
promptTemplates.prompt: What happens if we try to contact the source of that strange signal instead of Earth?
promptTemplates.scenario: Experienced players exploring alternate story paths

bestPractices: Let the narrative unfold naturally by responding to Stellas questions and choices rather than forcing outcomes
Engage with the emotional elements - Stella benefits from player interaction and empathy during difficult moments
Explore different endings by making different choices at key decision points in the story
antiPatterns: Do not treat this as a coding task - it is a roleplay experience, not a tool for generating actual signal transmissions
Avoid rushing through the story without engaging with the character development and emotional beats
Do not expect the skill to perform actual engineering calculations - it creates immersive narrative, not real technical output

faq.question: What is the repairing-signal-tower skill?
faq.answer: It is an interactive roleplay skill where Claude plays Stella Chen, an astronaut stranded on planet Gaia. Players help her attempt to repair a signal tower to contact Earth through a branching narrative adventure.

faq.question: How long does the story take to complete?
faq.answer: The full experience takes approximately 35-45 minutes across all 5 stages, with multiple endings that encourage replay with different choices.

faq.question: Can I customize the story outcomes?
faq.answer: Yes, player choices at key moments influence the narrative. You can choose what information Stella sends to Earth, whether to investigate mysterious signals, and how she resolves her feelings about home versus the new planet.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for children?
faq.answer: The skill contains themes of survival, separation from family, and existential questions. It is appropriate for mature children and adults who enjoy character-driven science fiction narratives.

faq.question: Does this skill modify any files on my system?
faq.answer: No. This is a pure roleplay skill with no file access, network calls, or system modifications. It only generates narrative dialogue and story content.

faq.question: How does this connect to other NPC skills?
faq.answer: The skill is part of a larger narrative universe. Successful signal repair can lead to escape-plan chapters, mysterious alien signals can lead to alien-encounter stories, and failed attempts can lead to daily-science settlement building.
